title: bupleurum falcatum subsp. scorzonerifolium
intro: Bupleurum falcatum subsp. scorzonerifolium, also known as Chinese thunder of heaven or kuchimgari, is a subspecies of the Bupleurum falcatum plant, a type of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It has been traditionally used in East Asian medicine, particularly in Chinese and Korean herbalism, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Bupleurum falcatum subsp. scorzonerifolium include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate rheumatic pain, and improve liver function, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, from respiratory problems to digestive disorders.
